codeReview.summary: The repository correctly preserves alarm identity and enabled-state toggles for the fields it currently stores, but the feature does not implement persistence for the required schedule and validation-state models described in the feature contract. The shipped tests also miss those gaps, so alarms cannot round-trip repeat schedules and there is no separate ValidationState model/repository mapping as requested.

issues.description: WakeAlarm only persists selected accessory IDs and scalar alarm fields; it has no relationship or embedded field for WakeAlarmSchedule/WeekdaySchedule, so repeat schedules are never saved or reopened even though the feature description explicitly requires schedule models and the expected behavior says all supported alarm configuration fields must round-trip.

WakeAlarmRepository save/update paths only read and write the WakeAlarm row. They never create, load, or synchronize a WakeAlarmSchedule record, so editing or fetching an alarm cannot preserve repeat-day configuration as required by the feature scope.

The implementation replaces the required validation-state model with a raw-string enum stored directly on WakeAlarm. The feature called for separate domain and persistence models for validation state; without a dedicated model/repository mapping, per-alarm validation metadata cannot evolve independently from the alarm record as specified.

Repository coverage claims to validate round-trip of all supported configuration fields, but the tests assert only scalar alarm properties and accessory IDs. They never create or verify a persisted WakeAlarmSchedule or a dedicated validation-state model, which allowed the missing feature requirements to slip through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the swiftui-feature-worker startup procedure only partially: the transcript read mission docs, AGENTS, services, and features, but failed to read the repo-local architecture document from the correct path before coding.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session 20d934ff-cce3-4ab8-9411-f81f12aa4f48 shows a failed read of /Users/jasoncantor/.factory/library/architecture.md, while the skill requires reading .factory/library/architecture.md in the repo before touching code.

summary: Review failed: alarm identity/enabled-state persistence works for the stored fields, but the feature contract is not fully met because repeat schedules and a dedicated validation-state model were not actually persisted or exercised by tests.
